published_prior_art.cite: ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 gives nonunique-bridge functional invariance; BalkePearl1997 gives finite latent LP bounds; neither names the K-latent-realizable bridge-null cone

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship: the generic open-class separation has no valid concrete non-corner witness because Exhibit 9.2's displayed latent law does not realize the claimed bridge segment.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The promised Farkas multiplier and distinct sharp ATE sets are not actually computed and verified from the Section 6 primitives; the exhibit leaves the finite multiplier inequalities unfinished.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: In Exhibit 9.2, with W|U rows (1/2,1/2,0) and (0,1/2,1/2), h_s=(1/2+s,1/2-s,1/2+s) induces latent means 1/2 in both U states, not the stated 1/4 and 3/4; the displayed loading also vanishes for the Exhibit 9.1 W marginal.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The proposed Q nonmembership certificate uses equality of latent outcome means, but that equality is not one of the locked R_K restrictions, so it cannot certify nonliftability in the stated model.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: As written, the null-cone certificate is mostly the generic finite-dimensional polyhedral projection/Farkas theorem applied after unfolding L_K and bridge feasibility; it needs a nonroutine characterization to clear flagship.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Exhibit 9.2 with a fully solved K=2 latent completion pair: specify all observable cells, latent tables, bridge values, ATE loadings, and a Farkas multiplier whose inequalities are enumerated over every nonnegative cell.

Strengthen Conjecture 1 beyond generic Farkas projection by giving an explicit support-capped algorithm, dimension/complexity bound, or closed-form certificate class that is not just LP feasibility in new notation.

Add a boundary corollary showing exactly when K_lat,K equals K_alg and when the inclusion is strict, with the condition stated in observable/latent-table primitives rather than as the definition of liftability.

recommended_next_step: Revise the finite witness first; without a valid K=2 same-quotient/different-sharp-set exhibit, the flagship target is not reachable.
